forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KWJ3S972X06D1TKBDBCVB3SD
+- 01KWJ3VNKRFPHF36XXH740CWZM
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9880'
+title: 'Diagnostics op: settle engine, scopes, dependents fold-in'
+---
+## What
+Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/{diagnose,settle,record}.rs`. `diagnostics(scope:)` with `.workingTree` (git status: modified+untracked+staged), `.file(glob)`, `.sha(range)` scopes; paths absolutized, confined to root, filtered to diagnosable extensions. For each target: syncOpen + pullDiagnostics, then the settle engine — subscribe to the session diagnostics stream, seed from cache, `Settled` after 300ms quiescence, `Pending` at 5s hard timeout (injectable clock). Dependents: one-hop inbound via blastRadius; only **broken** dependents (≥1 error/warning) fold in, ranked errors-then-warnings, capped at 100 per report. `pending: true` when settle timed out or session `!isReady`. `DiagnosticsReport { records, counts, pending }`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Manual-clock settle: an update at t+200ms restarts the quiescence window; silence to t+300ms → Settled; continuous updates → Pending at 5s
+- [ ] Clean dependents are excluded; broken dependents appear ranked after queried files
+- [ ] Server running but not ready → report flagged pending even if empty
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/DiagnosticsTests.swift`: settle timing matrix with manual clock; scope resolution against a temp git repo fixture; dependents fold-in with seeded call edges; pending flag truth table
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter DiagnosticsTests` → all pass
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

 ---
 ## What
-Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/{diagnose,settle,record}.rs`. `diagnostics(scope:)` with `.workingTree` (git status: modified+untracked+staged), `.file(glob)`, `.sha(range)` scopes; paths absolutized, confined to root, filtered to diagnosable extensions. For each target: syncOpen + pullDiagnostics, then the settle engine — subscribe to the session diagnostics stream, seed from cache, `Settled` after 300ms quiescence, `Pending` at 5s hard timeout (injectable clock). Dependents: one-hop inbound via blastRadius; only **broken** dependents (≥1 error/warning) fold in, ranked errors-then-warnings, capped at 100 per report. `pending: true` when settle timed out or session `!isReady`. `DiagnosticsReport { records, counts, pending }`.
+Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/{diagnose,settle,record}.rs`. `diagnostics(scope:severity:)` with `.workingTree` (git status: modified+untracked+staged), `.file(glob)`, `.sha(range)` scopes and a **severity floor parameter defaulting to `.warning`** (records below the floor excluded; lowering to `.hint` includes everything); paths absolutized, confined to root, filtered to diagnosable extensions. For each target: syncOpen + pullDiagnostics, then the settle engine — subscribe to the session diagnostics stream, seed from cache, `Settled` after 300ms quiescence, `Pending` at 5s hard timeout (injectable clock). Dependents: one-hop inbound via blastRadius; only **broken** dependents (≥1 error/warning) fold in, ranked errors-then-warnings, capped at 100 per report. `pending: true` when settle timed out or session `!isReady`. `DiagnosticsReport { records, counts, pending }`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] Manual-clock settle: an update at t+200ms restarts the quiescence window; silence to t+300ms → Settled; continuous updates → Pending at 5s
+- [ ] Severity floor: hint/info diagnostics excluded at the default `.warning` floor, included when the floor is lowered to `.hint`
 - [ ] Clean dependents are excluded; broken dependents appear ranked after queried files
 - [ ] Server running but not ready → report flagged pending even if empty

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/DiagnosticsTests.swift`: settle timing matrix with manual clock; scope resolution against a temp git repo fixture; dependents fold-in with seeded call edges; pending flag truth table
+- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/DiagnosticsTests.swift`: settle timing matrix with manual clock; severity floor filtering; scope resolution against a temp git repo fixture; dependents fold-in with seeded call edges; pending flag truth table
 - [ ] Run `swift test --filter DiagnosticsTests` → all pass

 ## Workflow

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,20 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: wballard
+  id: 01kwph51fkmvsxy870d7fne6dg
+  text: |-
+    Research complete. Key findings:
+
+    - Rust reference found at /Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer/crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/src/{diagnose,settle,record,config}.rs. Scope resolution (git status/glob/sha) lives in the *caller* (crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/.../diagnostics/mod.rs), not in the diagnostics crate itself, and uses git2 (libgit2) — this Swift port has no git2 dependency, so scope resolution will shell out to the `git` CLI via Process (matching ProcessLanguageServerConnection's spawn style), documented as a deliberate divergence.
+    - LspSession (Sources/CodeContextKit/LSP/LspSession.swift) already has everything needed: syncOpen, pullDiagnostics, diagnosticUpdates() (AsyncStream, multi-subscriber), diagnostics(for:), isReady. No isRunning — "not running" is modeled as `session: LspSession<Connection>? == nil`, matching LiveOpsCore's convention.
+    - DiagnosticSeverity already exists (LSPTypes.swift): .error=1/.warning=2/.information=3/.hint=4 — severity floor = "rawValue <= floor.rawValue".
+    - BlastRadiusOps.blastRadius(store:file:symbol:maxHops:) (Ops/BlastRadius.swift) is the one-hop-dependents primitive; call with maxHops:1, symbol:nil, collect hop symbols' filePath excluding self.
+    - No git-status precedent in this codebase at all (grep came up empty) — new pattern, modeled on ProcessLanguageServerConnection's Process/Pipe spawn style but as one-shot commands (not the persistent raw-fd reader).
+    - Clock injection pattern confirmed: `any Clock<Duration> = ContinuousClock()` (LSPIndexWorker, ProcessLanguageServerConnection); tests use Tests/CodeContextKitTests/Support/ManualClock.swift with `waitForWaiter()`/`advance(by:)`. For the settle engine's absolute hard-deadline + resettable debounce-deadline race, existentials can't expose `.now`/Instant cleanly, so Settle's internal race functions are generic over `<C: Clock>` instead of using `any Clock<Duration>` — the public entry point still takes `any Clock<Duration> = ContinuousClock()` for ergonomics and forwards into the generic implementation (relying on Swift's implicit existential opening, SE-0352).
+    - AsyncStream cancellation semantics for racing stream-updates against timers were not something I wanted to gamble on, so Settle uses a small cancellation-safe `UpdateMailbox` actor (modeled directly on ManualClock's own waiter/continuation pattern already trusted in this codebase) fed by a persistent unstructured drain Task, rather than racing `AsyncStream.next()` directly inside a TaskGroup.
+
+    Proceeding with TDD: writing DiagnosticsTests.swift first (failing), then implementing Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-04T12:20:16.499107+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KWJ3S972X06D1TKBDBCVB3SD
 - 01KWJ3VNKRFPHF36XXH740CWZM

+## What
Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/{diagnose,settle,record}.rs`. `diagnostics(scope:severity:)` with `.workingTree` (git status: modified+untracked+staged), `.file(glob)`, `.sha(range)` scopes and a **severity floor parameter defaulting to `.warning`** (records below the floor excluded; lowering to `.hint` includes everything); paths absolutized, confined to root, filtered to diagnosable extensions. For each target: syncOpen + pullDiagnostics, then the settle engine — subscribe to the session diagnostics stream, seed from cache, `Settled` after 300ms quiescence, `Pending` at 5s hard timeout (injectable clock). Dependents: one-hop inbound via blastRadius; only **broken** dependents (≥1 error/warning) fold in, ranked errors-then-warnings, capped at 100 per report. `pending: true` when settle timed out or session `!isReady`. `DiagnosticsReport { records, counts, pending }`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manual-clock settle: an update at t+200ms restarts the quiescence window; silence to t+300ms → Settled; continuous updates → Pending at 5s
- [ ] Severity floor: hint/info diagnostics excluded at the default `.warning` floor, included when the floor is lowered to `.hint`
- [ ] Clean dependents are excluded; broken dependents appear ranked after queried files
- [ ] Server running but not ready → report flagged pending even if empty

## Tests
- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/DiagnosticsTests.swift`: settle timing matrix with manual clock; severity floor filtering; scope resolution against a temp git repo fixture; dependents fold-in with seeded call edges; pending flag truth table
- [ ] Run `swift test --filter DiagnosticsTests` → all pass

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-05 09:49)

Scope: HEAD~1..HEAD (commit c7ddd5a). One finding from the engine's report was dropped per the never-refactor-existing-tests rule (it asked to extract a shared test fixture helper into a module also imported by the pre-existing `CallGraphOpsTests.swift`); the remaining 5 confirmed findings are recorded below. The engine's own duplication-analysis pass already self-refuted a 6th candidate finding that would have suggested converting `UpdateMailbox` back to an actor — correctly, since that would reintroduce the cancellation-handling deadlock fixed this session (an actor's `onCancel` can only be reached via `await`, forcing a detached `Task` that breaks `TaskGroup`'s structured-concurrency guarantee that a cancelled child actually finishes before the group returns). Do not apply that reversion.

- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:65` — Nested loops: a for loop iterating over `await oneHopDependents()` results is nested inside another for loop over targets. Extract the dependent collection logic into a helper or use flatMap to consolidate: `var collected = Set(targets.flatMap { target in await oneHopDependents(store: store, filePath: target) })`.
- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:85` — Nested loops: a for loop over `hop.symbols` is nested inside another for loop over `radius.hops`, combined with a `where` filter. Use flatMap to flatten: `let files = Set(radius.hops.flatMap { $0.symbols.filter { $0.filePath != filePath }.map { $0.filePath } })`.
- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:106` — Reimplements an existing helper. The doc comment states this function 'mirrors `LiveOpsCore`'s identically-named private helper' — the same functionality already exists elsewhere. Either reuse `LiveOpsCore`'s implementation (if access level permits) or extract a shared internal helper both modules can call instead of reimplementing it.
- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Settle.swift:143` — Deep nesting (4+ levels): the `while true` loop's `switch` statement nests a `for` loop inside the `.updates` case, creating 4 levels of nesting. Extract the `.updates` case handling into a separate helper function to reduce nesting depth.
- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/UpdateMailbox.swift:34` — The `record` and `markClosed` methods are near-verbatim duplicates of the lock-modify-extract-unlock-resume pattern, differing only in the state mutation (`queued.append(update)` vs `isClosed = true`). Extract a shared helper parameterized by the state mutation (e.g. `private func updateStateAndWakeWaiters(_ mutate: () -> Void)`) and call it from both methods with their respective mutations passed as closures.

\ No newline at end of file

+## What
+Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/{diagnose,settle,record}.rs`. `diagnostics(scope:severity:)` with `.workingTree` (git status: modified+untracked+staged), `.file(glob)`, `.sha(range)` scopes and a **severity floor parameter defaulting to `.warning`** (records below the floor excluded; lowering to `.hint` includes everything); paths absolutized, confined to root, filtered to diagnosable extensions. -## What
Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/{diagnose,settle,record}.rs`. `diagnostics(scope:severity:)` with `.workingTree` (git status: modified+untracked+staged), `.file(glob)`, `.sha(range)` scopes and a **severity floor parameter defaulting to `.warning`** (records below the floor excluded; lowering to `.hint` includes everything); paths absolutized, confined to root, filtered to diagnosable extensions. For each target: syncOpen + pullDiagnostics, then the settle engine — subscribe to the session diagnostics stream, seed from cache, `Settled` after 300ms quiescence, `Pending` at 5s hard timeout (injectable clock). Dependents: one-hop inbound via blastRadius; only **broken** dependents (≥1 error/warning) fold in, ranked errors-then-warnings, capped at 100 per report. `pending: true` when settle timed out or session `!isReady`. `DiagnosticsReport { records, counts, pending }`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manual-clock settle: an update at t+200ms restarts the quiescence window; silence to t+300ms → Settled; continuous updates → Pending at 5s
- [ ] Severity floor: hint/info diagnostics excluded at the default `.warning` floor, included when the floor is lowered to `.hint`
- [ ] Clean dependents are excluded; broken dependents appear ranked after queried files
- [ ] Server running but not ready → report flagged pending even if empty

## Tests
- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/DiagnosticsTests.swift`: settle timing matrix with manual clock; severity floor filtering; scope resolution against a temp git repo fixture; dependents fold-in with seeded call edges; pending flag truth table
- [ ] Run `swift test --filter DiagnosticsTests` → all pass

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-05 09:49)

Scope: HEAD~1..HEAD (commit c7ddd5a). One finding from the engine's report was dropped per the never-refactor-existing-tests rule (it asked to extract a shared test fixture helper into a module also imported by the pre-existing `CallGraphOpsTests.swift`); the remaining 5 confirmed findings are recorded below. The engine's own duplication-analysis pass already self-refuted a 6th candidate finding that would have suggested converting `UpdateMailbox` back to an actor — correctly, since that would reintroduce the cancellation-handling deadlock fixed this session (an actor's `onCancel` can only be reached via `await`, forcing a detached `Task` that breaks `TaskGroup`'s structured-concurrency guarantee that a cancelled child actually finishes before the group returns). Do not apply that reversion.

- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:65` — Nested loops: a for loop iterating over `await oneHopDependents()` results is nested inside another for loop over targets. Extract the dependent collection logic into a helper or use flatMap to consolidate: `var collected = Set(targets.flatMap { target in await oneHopDependents(store: store, filePath: target) })`.
- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:85` — Nested loops: a for loop over `hop.symbols` is nested inside another for loop over `radius.hops`, combined with a `where` filter. Use flatMap to flatten: `let files = Set(radius.hops.flatMap { $0.symbols.filter { $0.filePath != filePath }.map { $0.filePath } })`.
- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:106` — Reimplements an existing helper. The doc comment states this function 'mirrors `LiveOpsCore`'s identically-named private helper' — the same functionality already exists elsewhere. Either reuse `LiveOpsCore`'s implementation (if access level permits) or extract a shared internal helper both modules can call instead of reimplementing it.
- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Settle.swift:143` — Deep nesting (4+ levels): the `while true` loop's `switch` statement nests a `for` loop inside the `.updates` case, creating 4 levels of nesting. Extract the `.updates` case handling into a separate helper function to reduce nesting depth.
- [ ] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/UpdateMailbox.swift:34` — The `record` and `markClosed` methods are near-verbatim duplicates of the lock-modify-extract-unlock-resume pattern, differing only in the state mutation (`queued.append(update)` vs `isClosed = true`). Extract a shared helper parameterized by the state mutation (e.g. `private func updateStateAndWakeWaiters(_ mutate: () -> Void)`) and call it from both methods with their respective mutations passed as closures.

\ No newline at end of file
+## What
Create `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/` — port of `crates/swissarmyhammer-diagnostics/{diagnose,settle,record}.rs`. `diagnostics(scope:severity:)` with `.workingTree` (git status: modified+untracked+staged), `.file(glob)`, `.sha(range)` scopes and a **severity floor parameter defaulting to `.warning`** (records below the floor excluded; lowering to `.hint` includes everything); paths absolutized, confined to root, filtered to diagnosable extensions. For each target: syncOpen + pullDiagnostics, then the settle engine — subscribe to the session diagnostics stream, seed from cache, `Settled` after 300ms quiescence, `Pending` at 5s hard timeout (injectable clock). Dependents: one-hop inbound via blastRadius; only **broken** dependents (≥1 error/warning) fold in, ranked errors-then-warnings, capped at 100 per report. `pending: true` when settle timed out or session `!isReady`. `DiagnosticsReport { records, counts, pending }`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manual-clock settle: an update at t+200ms restarts the quiescence window; silence to t+300ms → Settled; continuous updates → Pending at 5s
- [ ] Severity floor: hint/info diagnostics excluded at the default `.warning` floor, included when the floor is lowered to `.hint`
- [ ] Clean dependents are excluded; broken dependents appear ranked after queried files
- [ ] Server running but not ready → report flagged pending even if empty

## Tests
- [ ] `Tests/CodeContextKitTests/DiagnosticsTests.swift`: settle timing matrix with manual clock; severity floor filtering; scope resolution against a temp git repo fixture; dependents fold-in with seeded call edges; pending flag truth table
- [ ] Run `swift test --filter DiagnosticsTests` → all pass

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

## Review Findings (2026-07-05 09:49)

Scope: HEAD~1..HEAD (commit c7ddd5a). One finding from the engine's report was dropped per the never-refactor-existing-tests rule (it asked to extract a shared test fixture helper into a module also imported by the pre-existing `CallGraphOpsTests.swift`); the remaining 5 confirmed findings are recorded below. The engine's own duplication-analysis pass already self-refuted a 6th candidate finding that would have suggested converting `UpdateMailbox` back to an actor — correctly, since that would reintroduce the cancellation-handling deadlock fixed this session (an actor's `onCancel` can only be reached via `await`, forcing a detached `Task` that breaks `TaskGroup`'s structured-concurrency guarantee that a cancelled child actually finishes before the group returns). Do not apply that reversion.

- [x] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:65` — Nested loops: a for loop iterating over `await oneHopDependents()` results is nested inside another for loop over targets. Extract the dependent collection logic into a helper or use flatMap to consolidate: `var collected = Set(targets.flatMap { target in await oneHopDependents(store: store, filePath: target) })`.
  - Fixed: `resolveDependents` keeps the outer `for target in targets` loop (needed for the sequential `await`), but the inner nested loop is now `collected.formUnion(dependents.filter { !targetSet.contains($0) })` — no nested loop, same dedup/exclusion behavior.
- [x] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:85` — Nested loops: a for loop over `hop.symbols` is nested inside another for loop over `radius.hops`, combined with a `where` filter. Use flatMap to flatten: `let files = Set(radius.hops.flatMap { $0.symbols.filter { $0.filePath != filePath }.map { $0.filePath } })`.
  - Fixed: `oneHopDependents` now uses `radius.hops.flatMap { hop in hop.symbols.filter { $0.filePath != filePath }.map(\.filePath) }` then `Set(files).sorted()` — fully synchronous, no nested loop, same result.
- [x] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Diagnose.swift:106` — Reimplements an existing helper. The doc comment states this function 'mirrors `LiveOpsCore`'s identically-named private helper' — the same functionality already exists elsewhere. Either reuse `LiveOpsCore`'s implementation (if access level permits) or extract a shared internal helper both modules can call instead of reimplementing it.
  - Fixed: moved `relativeFilePath(fromURI:rootDirectory:)` into `Sources/CodeContextKit/Index/RelativePath.swift` as `RelativePath.relativeFilePath(fromURI:rootDirectory:)` (alongside the existing `RelativePath.of`/`RelativePath.isSafeRelativePath` shared helpers), removed both private duplicates (Diagnose.swift and LiveOpsCore.swift), and updated all 4 call sites (1 in Diagnose.swift, 3 in LiveOpsCore.swift) to call the shared helper.
- [x] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/Settle.swift:143` — Deep nesting (4+ levels): the `while true` loop's `switch` statement nests a `for` loop inside the `.updates` case, creating 4 levels of nesting. Extract the `.updates` case handling into a separate helper function to reduce nesting depth.
  - Fixed: extracted `applyWatchedUpdates(_:watched:into:)` (a private static func taking the updates, the watched set, and `inout` state) out of the `.updates` case; `settleStream` now just calls it and restarts the debounce deadline based on its return value. Debounce/hard-timeout racing logic and timing semantics are unchanged — confirmed by the full settle timing matrix in `DiagnosticsTests` still passing (quiescence restart at t+200ms, settle at t+300ms of silence, pending at the 5s hard timeout, unwatched updates ignored).
- [x] `Sources/CodeContextKit/Diagnostics/UpdateMailbox.swift:34` — The `record` and `markClosed` methods are near-verbatim duplicates of the lock-modify-extract-unlock-resume pattern, differing only in the state mutation (`queued.append(update)` vs `isClosed = true`). Extract a shared helper parameterized by the state mutation (e.g. `private func updateStateAndWakeWaiters(_ mutate: () -> Void)` or similar) and call it from both methods with their respective mutations passed as closures.
  - Fixed: extracted `mutateStateAndResumeWaiters(_ mutate: () -> Void)`; `record(_:)` and `markClosed()` are now one-line calls into it. `UpdateMailbox` remains a plain `NSLock`-guarded class (`final class UpdateMailbox: @unchecked Sendable`) — NOT converted to an actor, per the deliberate cancellation-handling design constraint noted above.

\ No newline at end of file
